title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras is appealing a seven-game suspension for his involvement in a confrontation with the Nationals.
Contreras is dealing with emotional stress from recent Venezuelan earthquakes, which killed nearly 3,000 people.
Contreras apologized for his on-field behavior, attributing it to emotional distress from personal and national events.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman has a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves over 25.2 innings in 2026.
Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts over 88.2 innings; All-Star game held in Philadelphia.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but not surprise over Celtics trading Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Pierce encouraged Brown to use the perceived lack of respect as motivation to further his basketball career.
Jaylen Brown criticized Celtics' Brad Stevens for his role in the trade, expressing feelings of disrespect.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the Fourth of July with a parade and a reading of the Declaration of Independence.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular began with a drone show at the Hatch Memorial Shell.
Mayor Michelle Wu participated by attending the Declaration of Independence reading outside the Old State House.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS and home runs over the last 30 days with a .977 OPS and seven home runs.
Durbin took over third base after contract talks with Alex Bregman faltered, improving significantly since adjusting his hitting strategy.
The Red Sox have team control of Durbin until 2031, offering salary cap flexibility for potential future free agency moves.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman broke the MLB record for relief pitcher strikeouts, achieving his 1,364th during a game against the Angels.
Chapman surpassed Hoyt Wilhelm's record in fewer innings, needing 847 compared to Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's milestone with a video montage; discussions suggest he may be traded by the deadline.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Allstate's 2026 report lists Boston as the most collision-prone U.S. city, with an average of 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts, rank among the top 10 riskiest cities for drivers, with averages of 5.14 and 5.18 years between collisions.
Providence, Rhode Island, ranks 194th in collision frequency, while Brownsville, Texas, has the lowest rate with nearly 15 years between crashes.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, was arrested for leading a 105 mph wrong-way chase across the Bourne Bridge.
Deleon faces over a dozen charges including speeding, resisting arrest, and assault with a dangerous weapon.
The pursuit ended when Deleon barricaded himself at home; he is held without bail pending a court hearing.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a juvenile suspect in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the June 10 armed robbery on West Ninth Street.
Community support followed the robbery, with events attended by Mayor Michelle Wu and Councilor Ed Flynn.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is exploring new NBA team options and has notified the Lakers of his decision to leave.
Rich Paul mentioned 'Brad + Tatum' on a podcast as a potential but unlikely landing spot for James.
Boston's aggressive roster retooling includes adding Mitchell Robinson and Paul George to strengthen their NBA Finals chances.
